clusters.title: After US attacks, is Iran's navy on the brink?

items.title: After US attacks, is Iran's navy on the brink?
items.standfirst: On Wednesday, American Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters. The Pentagon says one of the main aims of the US-Israeli war against Iran, launched Saturday, is to wipe out the country's navy, and on Wednesday the US claimed to have “effectively neutralised Iran’s major naval presence”. In this edition of Truth or Fake, we look at what we know about the state of the Iranian navy now.

In this edition of Truth or Fake, we look at what we know about the state of the Iranian navy now.

items.title: US submarine torpedoed Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Pentagon says
items.standfirst: A US submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday, touting the strike as evidence of America's global reach in its war on Iran. "An American submarine sunk an Iranian warship that thought it was safe in international waters. Instead, it was sunk by a torpedo," Hegseth told a news conference. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.

clusters.title: Rep. Tony Gonzales advances to runoff amid affair allegations with aide
clusters.neutral_headline: Rep Tony Gonzales Advances to Runoff

items.title: Rep. Tony Gonzales advances to runoff amid affair allegations with aide
items.standfirst: Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales of Texas will face gun rights activist Brandon Herrera in a runoff election after neither candidate secured more than 50% of the votes in their primary contest. Gonzales has been accused of having an affair with an aide who later died by suicide, which he denies. CBS News reporter Karen Hua has more from Houston.
items.content: Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales of Texas will face gun rights activist Brandon Herrera in a runoff election after neither candidate secured more than 50% of the votes in their primary contest. Gonzales has been accused of having an affair with an aide who later died by suicide, which he denies. CBS News reporter Karen Hua has more from Houston.

items.title: GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales heads to a runoff in Texas amid a new ethics probe in the House
items.standfirst: Texas Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales has faced increasing pressure from his party to resign or drop out of his race after allegations of an affair with a staffer.
items.content: Texas Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales has faced increasing pressure from his party to resign or drop out of his race after allegations of an affair with a staffer.(Image credit: Samuel Corum)

items.title: Scandal-plagued Rep. Tony Gonzales forced to runoff
items.standfirst: Gonzales, who was accused of having an affair with a staffer who later died by suicide, will face influencer Brandon Herrera in a May 26 runoff.
items.content: Scandal-embroiled Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas) on Tuesday was forced into a runoff election after weeks of backlash to reports alleging he had an affair with a former staffer, who later took her own life. He will face Brandon Herrera — a 2nd Amendment influencer who goes by “TheAKGuy” on social media — in late May. That sets them up for yet another expensive and prolonged contest like they experienced in 2024, when Gonzales prevailed by one percentage point. Gonzales, who has represented the west Texas district since 2021, has faced calls to resign from several of his GOP colleagues after new evidence emerged of his alleged affair. Gonzales previously denied the affair and repeatedly said he would not step down.Gonzales’ South Texas district favors Republicans, but could potentially become competitive should Hispanic voters sour on the GOP this cycle or stay home. Even with the scandal, House GOP leadership declined to rescind their endorsements of Gonzales and were content to wait and see how the voters decided. “There’s a primary there in less than a week, these things will play out,” Speaker Mike Johnson said recently.

ai_summary: Rep Tony Gonzales will face Brandon Herrera in a runoff election
Gonzales has been accused of having an affair with a staffer who died by suicide
The allegations have led to increased pressure on Gonzales to resign
Gonzales denies the allegations and will proceed to the runoff
The runoff election is scheduled for May 26

items.title: Spain denies US claim of military cooperation on Iran as rift deepens
items.standfirst: Spain hardened its stance towards Washington on Wednesday over the use of Spanish bases in operations against Iran, defying US President Donald Trump’s threats of trade reprisals and deepening the friction between the NATO allies. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez repeated his call of “no to the war,” insisting Madrid would not be complicit in the conflict, even as the White House made claims that Spain had agreed to cooperate with the US military.
items.content: Spain hardened its stance towards Washington on Wednesday over the use of Spanish bases in operations against Iran, defying US President Donald Trump’s threats of trade reprisals and deepening the friction between the NATO allies. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez repeated his call of “no to the war,” insisting Madrid would not be complicit in the conflict, even as the White House made claims that Spain had agreed to cooperate with the US military.

items.title: Spain accuses US of "playing Russian roulette" with millions of lives in Iran
items.standfirst: Spain's Prime Minister doubled down on his criticism of Washington on March 3rd, a day after Donald Trump threatened to cut off all trade with the country. That itself came in response to Spain's refusal to allow the use of military bases shared with the US in its territory for Washington's continued war with Israel on Iran. Pedro Sanchez warned Trump he was playing Russian roulette with the lives of millions, and urged the world to learn from history.
items.content: Spain's Prime Minister doubled down on his criticism of Washington on March 3rd, a day after Donald Trump threatened to cut off all trade with the country. That itself came in response to Spain's refusal to allow the use of military bases shared with the US in its territory for Washington's continued war with Israel on Iran. Pedro Sanchez warned Trump he was playing Russian roulette with the lives of millions, and urged the world to learn from history.

items.title: Spain's Sanchez defies Trump trade threat, compares Iran war to ‘Russian roulette’
items.standfirst: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez doubled down Wednesday, criticising US and Israeli military actions in Iran and refusing to bow to trade pressure from Washington. In a defiant televised address, Sanchez warned that the escalating conflict in the Middle East was akin to “playing Russian roulette” with millions of lives.
items.content: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez doubled down Wednesday, criticising US and Israeli military actions in Iran and refusing to bow to trade pressure from Washington. In a defiant televised address, Sanchez warned that the escalating conflict in the Middle East was akin to “playing Russian roulette” with millions of lives.

items.title: ‘No to war’: Sánchez doubles down after Trump threat to cut off trade with Spain
items.standfirst: PM says his country will not be complicit in growing conflict in Middle East ‘simply out of fear of reprisals from someone’Middle East crisis – live updatesThe Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, has responded to Donald Trump’s extraordinary threat to cut off all trade with Spain over his government’s refusal to facilitate the US’s attacks against Iran, comparing the growing conflict in the Middle East to playing “Russian roulette with the destiny of millions”.Sánchez, who has been one of the most vociferous European critics of Israel’s conduct in Gaza, said his government’s position on the widening instability could be summed up in three words: “No to war.” Continue reading...

ai_summary: Live Nation is on trial for antitrust allegations related to its ticketing practices
The company is accused of using its market power to dominate the concert ticket ecosystem
The trial could result in a breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster
The case has implications for the concert industry and ticket prices
The trial is ongoing, with the Justice Department presenting its case against Live Nation
